name: Notion
security: Internal integrations have access only to pages/databases explicitly shared with them.
Public integrations use OAuth and require a redirect URI configured in your integration settings.
Tokens are scoped per workspace — rotate if a workspace's access is revoked.
Notion has no native webhooks for free; polling or third-party watchers are required (or use Notion Webhooks if on a paid plan).

patterns.name: Database as source of truth
patterns.description: Treat a Notion database as a lightweight CMS — query rows from your app.

patterns.name: Page creator
patterns.description: Programmatically create pages or database rows for capture flows (forms, AI summaries).

patterns.name: Append blocks
patterns.description: Append blocks to an existing page for logging or activity feeds.

triggers.name: Page created
triggers.description: Fires when a new page is created (requires polling or paid Notion Webhooks).

triggers.name: Page updated
triggers.description: Fires when a page's content changes.

triggers.name: Database row added
triggers.description: Fires when a new row is added to a database.

triggers.name: Database row updated
triggers.description: Fires when a database row's properties change.

actions.name: Create page
actions.description: Create a new page (standalone or as a child of a parent page/database).

actions.name: Update page properties
actions.description: Update properties (title, tags, dates, etc.) on an existing page.

actions.name: Append blocks to page
actions.description: Append paragraph, heading, list, or code blocks to a page.

actions.name: Query database
actions.description: Query a database with filters and sorts.

actions.name: Add database row
actions.description: Create a new row in a database with typed property values.

auth.description: OAuth 2.0 for public integrations distributed to other Notion workspaces.
